sections.paragraph:
αἰσθάνομαι fut. αἰσθήσῃ Pr 24:14, αἰσθηθήσεσθε Is 33:11, αἰσθανθήσεται 49:26; 2 aor. ᾐσθόμην, subj. αἴσθωμαι (Aeschyl.+).

① to be aware of someth. by means of the senses, notice (Cleanthes [s. 2, end]; Appian, Liby. 120 §568; TestJud 15:1; Tat. 17, 4) Dg 2:8 (cp. EpJer 19; 23).

② to have the capacity to discern and therefore understand what is not readily comprehensible, understand (X., Cyr. 1, 5, 4 al.; Pr 17:10; 24:14; Ar.; Iren. 1, 4, 1 [Harv. I 32, 7]) ῥῆμα Lk 9:45 (opp. ἀγνοέω). γνώμην B 2:9. W. ὅτι foll. (Ar. 12, 8; Dio Chrys. 52 [69], 2; Is 49:26) 6:18. αἰσθάνεσθε (you) notice w. indir. quest. foll. (Epict. 1, 6, 41; 3, 23, 16) 11:8; cp. 13:3.—Both meanings are included by Cleanthes [IV/III b.c.] Stoic. 1, p. 136, 1 (in Diog. L. 7, 172) in wordplay when he says to his pupil: οὐκ αἰσθάνομαι, ὅτι αἰσθάνῃ; ‘(Why, then, do) I not apprehend that you comprehend?’—B. 1020. DELG s.v. 1. ἀΐω. M-M. TW. Sv.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Cleanthes Cleanthes , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  3. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

foll. foll. = followed, following

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

indir. indir. = indirect

quest. quest. = question

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 29
